DataMaskingTip: When enabled, it obfuscates the transmission data in the RSign™ interface. In addition, it prevents users from downloading documents  except the initiator of the transaction. If enabled (Masking On), it will apply to all past and future transactions; if later disabled (Masking Off), it will disable for all past and future transactions.
migrateCompanyReview: An administrator changed your company information. Please review your account information and plan details listed below. If everything is looking correct, please click on the "Continue" button to start using the service. You only need to do this one time.

PrivateModeTip: Allows senders to restrict transaction data access to only the sender of the e-signature request. When Private Mode is enabled, the RSign™ system prevents non-senders from downloading or re-generating the transaction documents, including the original document, signature certificate, and email body by hiding the download options and masking transaction data within the envelopes.

AttachPDFForCCRecipientsTip: Allows sender to specify when PDF documents should be emailed to the Cc recipient. Unsigned – Only the unsigned PDF is emailed to the Cc recipient. Signed – Only the signed PDF is emailed to the Cc recipient. Unsigned and Signed – Both the unsigned and signed PDF is emailed to the Cc recipient. Notifications Only – Only notifications of sending and signing are emailed to Cc recipients, but not the PDF. Notifications and Signed – Notifications and both unsigned and signed PDFs are emailed to the CC recipient.

PersonalEnableiManageAccessTip:
If this setting is set to Y, RSign will restrict users to authenticate using SSO or iManage Server depending on the configuration in the iManage platform.
	If this setting is set to N, RSign users can login into RSign with their RSign credentials or authenticate via RSign SSO. Login via Other option to authenticate against the iManage server will not be allowed.

FooterTextParantedLink: pasauliniu mastu. Nei RPost, nei kiti šių paslaugų teikėjai neteikia teisinių konsultacijų. RPOST®, RMAIL®, RSIGN®, RFORMS™, (R)®, (R)EGISTERED EMAIL(TM), REGISTERED EMAIL(TM), REGISTERED RECEIPT(TM), LEGAL PROOF®, ESIGNOFF®, SIDENOTE® ir CERTIFIED EMAIL(TM) yra RPost registruotieji ir neregistruoti prekių ženklai Jungtinėse Valstijose ir registruotieji bei neregistruoti prekių ženklai pasaulio šalyse. Įvairios kitos įmonės ir produktai parodomi kaip logotipai ir prekių ženklai su šia svetaine susijusiuose interneto puslapiuose. Daugeliu arba visais atvejais atitinkamos įmonės pareiškia teises į šiuos ženklus kaip į registruotuosius prekių ženklus. Šių prekybinių pavadinimų ir logotipų naudojimas šioje svetainėje yra tik iliustracinės paskirties.

FilingFinalSignedDocumentsTip: Specifies how the final signed documents should be stored in iManage and NetDocuments. The versioning option stores the final signed document in the same folder as the original document but as a new version number. The new documents (no versioning) option allows you to choose the folder location within iManage or NetDocuments.

DisclaimerLocationTip: Specify the location of the disclaimer within the email body. Top of email body places the disclaimer above the  email message. Below email body places the disclaimer below the email message. Bottom of email places the text at the end of the email.
SendReminderUntilExpirationtoSenderTip: Specifies how frequently the senders should be reminded about expiring envelopes.
AllowSignerstoDownloadFinalContractToolT: When enabled, RSign allows the signer to download the final signed contract from the Completed folder of their RSign account by enabling the download icon. The download icon will also be enabled in the popup shown to the last signer in a multi-party e-Signature request, after they have finished submitting the document.

AccessAuthenticationTip: Specifies if and how the document should be protected before and after it is signed. The value of this setting is printed on the signature certificate to provide proof of secure delivery.  If “End-to-End” is selected, a password will be required for the recipient to view and sign the document and to view the final signed document. If “Required to Open Signed” is selected, a password will be needed to open the signed document. If “Signer Authentication” is selected the signer will be required to enter a six digit code sent to their email. If Select is selected, the user can access the document without entering any password.
lang_VerifyYourMobileFirstSigner: Step 1 of 2 is complete. You will receive a SMS message from RSign on your mobile to verify your mobile number. Please check the message  and confirm to complete step 2 of the transaction.
SendIndividualSignatureNotificationsTip: When enabled, the sender/Cc receives an email notification each time a recipient signs the document. When disabled, RSign™ notifies the sender/Cc once after all the signers have signed the document.
ClickOnCreateEnvelope: For sending an envelope to the selected record click on "Send Envelope".
lang_DocumentFormatChangedWarning: At least one signer input field is outside the margin of the page. Please review the signer input fields on pages X to move it inside the margin.
lang_BackConfirmationS1: Click on this to go to Page 2/3 after saving all the changes.
EnableSSOforiManageTip:
If this setting is set to Y, users under your company will be able to authenticate themselves against the iManage authentication server from the RSign Homepage by clicking the ‘Other options’ button to access the RSign application. Enabling this setting will show the same setting at the personal level to allow users to select if they prefer to access RSign with RSign credentials or iManage Authentication mechanism (SSO or iManage credentials). Moreover, if SSO is enabled at RPortal for your company and a domain is configured, new users navigating to RSign from iManage will be auto-created and placed under your Company account.
If this setting is set to N, new users navigating to RSign from iManage will be auto-created under a default account.
